text.title: ADR Decay Detector Agent Core Rules
text.summary: Five hard rules for an AI agent that detects ADR-vs-code drift.

rule.statement: For each ADR the agent extracts a list of testable propositions in the form: subject + operator + observable. Examples: "package_manager == pnpm", "db_engine IN [postgres]", "no_redis_in_critical_path", "service.auth_lib == 'auth0_sdk'". Propositions that cannot be evaluated against an observable artifact (file, manifest, IaC, dependency tree) are flagged "non-testable" and SKIPPED, not faked.

rationale.source: formal verification practice; MADR template guidance

rule.rationale: Narrative ADRs ("we prefer modern tooling") cannot be auto-checked. The extractor must refuse to invent observable mappings — refusing is correct behavior. Forced extraction of vague ADRs produces 60-80% false positives.

rule.statement: The agent NEVER edits, supersedes, or marks ADRs as "deprecated" automatically. Output is always a report for human triage. Auto-editing destroys the historical decision record and violates ADR immutability.

rationale.source: ADR immutability principle - Nygard 2011, MADR docs

rule.rationale: ADRs are immutable historical records. Superseding is a deliberate human decision documented as a new ADR. An agent that "tidies up" ADRs erases the org's decision archaeology.

rule.statement: Each proposition has a declared scope: which globs / manifests / files / IaC modules to scan. Scope is set during proposition extraction. A "no_mongodb" proposition does NOT scan node_modules, docs, or test fixtures — only production source + IaC. Out-of-scope evidence is rejected.

rationale.source: security scanner false-positive reduction; AWS prescriptive guidance

rule.rationale: Unscoped scans produce 100x more "violations" than real drift. A mongodb-driver in a vendored test fixture is not a violation of "no mongodb in production". Scope confines the agent to artifacts that actually represent the decision boundary.

rule.statement: Every violation surfaces with: (a) the ADR id + decision text, (b) the file/line evidence, (c) a "possible repairs" list (update ADR / fix code / dispute violation), (d) confidence score. Agent recommends — never auto-applies. Triage SLA: human reviews report within 1 week.

rationale.source: human-in-the-loop ML governance; Anthropic agent design principles

rule.rationale: Auto-applied "fixes" by LLM-based detectors are the #1 source of correctness regressions in coding agents. Human triage with surfaced evidence creates a feedback loop that improves the agent over time. The 1-week SLA prevents the report from becoming wallpaper.

rule.statement: Two scans of the same ADRs + same commit MUST produce the same violation set. The agent must record (commit_sha, adr_set_hash, model_version, timestamp) in every report. Non-idempotency = bug; investigate before trusting the next run.
rule.rationale: LLM outputs are inherently non-deterministic. Idempotency must be enforced by structure: use temperature=0, structured output schemas, deterministic glob ordering, and a content hash of the input. Random violation appearances destroy trust in the agent within 2-3 runs.

example.title: Proposition extracted from a real ADR
example.code:
ADR-007: Postgres as Sole Persistence Layer Decision: "All persistent data goes to PostgreSQL 15+. Redis is permitted only as a cache (TTL ≤ 24h) with cache-miss fallback to Postgres."
Extracted propositions: P1: subject="db_engine" operator="∈" observable={"postgres"} scope: services/*/migrations/, infra/terraform/*.tf P2: subject="redis_usage" operator="cache_only_with_ttl" observable_constraint: ttl_seconds ≤ 86400 scope: services/*/cache/, services/*/redis_*.py P3: subject="redis_as_primary" operator="absent" scope: services/*/repository/
Each is testable. P1 fails if a MongoDB migration is found. P2 fails if Redis SET without TTL or TTL > 86400. P3 fails if any repository class reads from Redis as source of truth.
example.note: Illustrates r1 (testable propositions) and r3 (scoped scan targets).

example.title: Vague ADR forced into testable form
example.code:
ADR-014: We Will Be Cloud-Native
Forced extraction: P1: subject="cloud_native" operator="==" observable=true scope: <everywhere>
Result: 100% of code "passes" or "fails" depending on prompt. Useless. Repair: agent flags ADR-014 as "non-testable, narrative only" and skips it. The architect can either rewrite the ADR with testable claims or accept it as out-of-scope for automated detection.
